macro_summary: Mega-cap tech earnings season is approaching a concentrated two-week window (MSFT July 29, AAPL July 30, NVDA Aug 26) against a backdrop of easing rate-hike fears after June CPI printed -0.4% MoM, dropping July hike odds from ~46% to below 17%. TSMC reports today, providing an immediate AI-demand read-through for the entire semiconductor complex. Geopolitical risk from the US-Iran Strait of Hormuz confrontation remains an inflation wildcard heading into the July 29 FOMC decision. AI export restriction easing and NVDA CEO's dismissal of Vera Rubin delay fears add incremental tailwinds for the AI supply chain.
screening_note: 22 independent screening runs analyzed over the past 24 hours. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. 7 tickers qualified. Ideas ranked by appearance frequency descending.
key_events: TODAY July 16: TSMC Q2 2026 earnings — consensus $3.83 EPS (+55% YoY), direct AI capex read-through
July 29: FOMC rate decision — hike probability collapsed to <17% on soft CPI
July 29 after close: MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings
July 30 after close: AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings
July 14: Barclays initiated SKHY (SK Hynix ADR) Overweight with $330 PT (~77% upside)
July 15: NVDA CEO Jensen Huang dismissed Vera Rubin hardware delay reports
July 15: AAPL hit all-time high +4%; Citi raised PT to $365 from $315
AI chip export restrictions eased by US government — structural tailwind for NVDA
US-Iran conflict: Strait of Hormuz blockade reinstated — energy inflation risk elevated

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 21 of 22 overnight screens — the strongest consensus in this digest. NVDA has pulled back ~11% from its 52-week high of $236.54 to ~$210, yet trades at just 22x forward P/E on 85% revenue growth and 75% gross margins — a PEG of ~0.6 that is historically cheap for the name. Today's TSMC print is an immediate same-session catalyst (AI chip demand confirmation), CEO Huang's July 15 dismissal of Vera Rubin delay fears removed a key overhang, and AI export restriction easing is a structural tailwind ahead of the August 26 Q2 FY2027 earnings re-rating event.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC earnings today July 16 (immediate read-through); NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26, 2026 after close
ideas.edge: 68-analyst consensus PT of $302.73 implies ~49% upside from ~$210; 22x forward P/E on 85% revenue growth (PEG ~0.6); revenue estimate revised up 6.69% in past 3 months; ~11% gap to 52-week high of $236.54 as near-term target
ideas.bull_case: A TSMC beat today confirms AI chip demand remains voracious, closing the gap to the 52-week high quickly; August 26 earnings deliver a beat-and-raise that triggers a full re-rating toward the $302 consensus PT on still-cheap fundamentals.
ideas.bear_case: Any Vera Rubin delay confirmation or softer-than-expected TSMC AI commentary reignites hardware concern; a hawkish FOMC surprise July 29 compresses high-multiple tech broadly before the August earnings event.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 21 of 22 overnight screens, making it a consensus AI-memory pure play despite its July 10 ADR debut. Barclays initiated Overweight on July 14 with a $330 PT implying ~77% upside from current ~$186 levels, and SK Hynix's own CEO has publicly warned of the worst-ever HBM supply shortage in 2027 that could persist beyond 2030. DRAM prices are projected up 50% on structural shortage dynamics driven by NVDA and hyperscaler AI buildouts, with total revenue already growing +40.82% YoY.
ideas.catalyst: Barclays Overweight initiation July 14 with $330 PT; SK Hynix Q2 earnings expected late July 2026; NVDA earnings August 26 as HBM demand confirmation
ideas.edge: Barclays PT of $330 vs. ~$186 current = ~77% upside; ADR IPO priced at $149, now ~$186 — still well below analyst target; DRAM/HBM shortage structural with CEO forecasting shortage lasting beyond 2030; options ecosystem now live
ideas.bull_case: Structural HBM shortage narrative validated at Q2 earnings accelerates institutional buying of the only US-listed pure-play AI memory stock; Barclays target acts as a near-term anchor pulling price higher with momentum.
ideas.bear_case: ADR trades at a 51% premium to underlying Korean shares — a compression risk if US-listed premium narrows; post-IPO volatility is extreme and liquidity in options remains thin, making position sizing critical with hard stop near the $150s IPO range.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 20 of 22 overnight screens with the highest conviction vote ratio in this digest (17 of 20 votes rated high). MSFT is down ~20% YTD and trades at ~20x forward earnings versus its historical ~30x average — a ~50% re-rating gap that could close on a July 29 earnings beat. The bar is intentionally low: Q4 consensus EPS of $4.24 is actually below Q3's $4.27 print, and Q3 delivered 18% revenue growth, making a beat-and-raise structurally probable if Azure AI demand holds.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29, 2026 after close; same-day FOMC decision creates a dual binary catalyst on July 29
ideas.edge: Trading at ~20x forward P/E vs. historical ~30x average — ~50% re-rating potential on a beat; analyst consensus PT ~$589–$592 implies ~56% upside from current price; EPS bar is below prior quarter ($4.24 vs. $4.27 Q3 actual)
ideas.bull_case: Azure AI capex ROI inflects positively in Q4 print, beat-and-raise unlocks valuation re-rating from 20x toward historical 30x norm in a single session; soft CPI-driven FOMC hold the same day removes the rate overhang simultaneously.
ideas.bear_case: Continued AI capex spending without near-term revenue payoff spooks the market on ROI concerns; a hawkish FOMC surprise the same day overwhelms any earnings beat and compounds the YTD drawdown.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 17 of 22 overnight screens with strong conviction (12 high votes). AAPL hit a new all-time high on July 15 (+4%) with freshly confirmed China AI approval (Qwen/Apple Intelligence integration) as a new growth driver, a Citi PT raise to $365 (+16% upside), and Q3 FY2026 earnings on July 30 featuring the strongest revenue guidance in years (14–17%). iPhone 18 cycle in September and the foldable iPhone represent stacked catalysts beyond the near-term print. Prior quarter showed 22% YoY iPhone growth and 17% total revenue growth to $111.2B.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026 after close; China AI approval (Qwen integration) already confirmed; iPhone 18 launch September 2026
ideas.edge: Citi PT raised to $365 implying ~16% upside; stock at all-time high with 14–17% revenue guidance for Q3; $100B fresh buyback program active; ~$4.56T market cap within 4% of reclaiming world's most valuable company status
ideas.bull_case: July 30 print beats on Services + China AI unlock unlocking a new revenue stream, validating ATH levels and pushing toward the $365 Citi target; iPhone 18 pre-order momentum provides a September follow-on catalyst.
ideas.bear_case: Stock entering earnings at all-time highs with elevated expectations — any China macro softness or Services deceleration creates a 'sell the news' dynamic; ATH entry risk is real and defines why options are preferred over outright stock.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 8 of 22 screens, primarily as a macro expression vehicle rather than a directional conviction trade. July 29 is a uniquely concentrated dual-catalyst date — FOMC rate decision and MSFT earnings after close simultaneously — creating a binary risk event for the Nasdaq-100. June CPI's -0.4% MoM print collapsed rate hike odds to below 17%, creating a macro tailwind; however, the Iran/energy inflation overhang and 9 of 18 Fed dots still projecting a 2026 hike keep the tail risk alive. VIX near 15.95 keeps option premiums reasonable for spread strategies.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC rate decision July 29, 2026 (dual catalyst with MSFT earnings same day); mega-cap earnings window July 29–Aug 26
ideas.edge: July hike probability collapsed from ~46% to <17% on June CPI beat — macro tailwind for QQQ; VIX ~15.95 makes option premiums affordable; two-week window of mega-cap earnings (MSFT July 29, AAPL July 30, NVDA Aug 26) creates sustained upside optionality if macro holds
ideas.bull_case: FOMC holds rates on July 29 (base case given <17% hike odds), MSFT beats same evening, and the Nasdaq re-rates higher into the AAPL and NVDA prints — a QQQ call spread expiring early August captures the full window.
ideas.bear_case: A hawkish FOMC surprise (hike or aggressive hawkish hold language) on July 29 combined with a MSFT capex disappointment could produce a sharp multi-percent leg lower in the Nasdaq; QQQ put spreads hedge this tail cleanly.

ideas.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 2 of 22 screens — at threshold — but carries outsized same-day relevance as TSMC reports Q2 2026 today. TSMC has beaten consensus EPS estimates in each of the last 4 quarters with an average positive surprise of 8.34%, and the Q3 guidance update due today is the most important AI capex data point of the current earnings season — a direct read-through for NVDA, the broader semiconductor complex, and AI infrastructure investment broadly. June revenue was already up 68% YoY, setting a high-visibility base.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings results today July 16; Q3 2026 guidance update — the AI capex swing factor for the entire semiconductor sector
ideas.edge: 4-quarter average earnings beat of +8.34%; June 2026 revenue already up +68% YoY; Barclays raised PT to $625 from $470; full-year 2026 revenue growth guided >30% in USD terms; high analyst target implies ~38% upside
ideas.bull_case: Beat-and-raise Q3 guide today confirms AI chip demand is accelerating into H2 2026, acting as a sector-wide positive catalyst for NVDA, SKHY, and the AI infrastructure trade simultaneously.
ideas.bear_case: At threshold appearance (2 of 22 runs), this is a today-only catalyst trade — any Q3 guide that disappoints on AI CoWoS/HBM capacity concerns could trigger a semiconductor-wide selloff and undermine the NVDA thesis.

ideas.name: Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 2 of 22 screens — at threshold — as a macro hedge idea rather than an alpha generator. The US-Iran Strait of Hormuz confrontation with no resolution catalyst in sight, combined with the Fed's revised PCE inflation forecast of 3.6% for 2026 driven primarily by energy, makes XLE a liquid portfolio hedge against the dominant macro risks overhanging the tech-heavy ideas in this digest. Brent surged 5.43% and WTI 4.37% in a single session of renewed hostilities; XLE gained 37% in Q1 2026 vs. SPY -4.63%.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC decision July 29 (hawkish hike/hold driven by energy-fueled inflation); ongoing US-Iran Strait of Hormuz escalation with no resolution timeline
ideas.edge: XLE gained 37% in Q1 2026 vs. SPY -4.63%; single Iran escalation session drove Brent +5.43%, WTI +4.37%; Fed PCE revised to 3.6% with energy as primary driver; Goldman base case $80/bbl Brent Q4 with binary upside skew on escalation
ideas.bull_case: Iran hostilities escalate further or FOMC delivers a hawkish surprise on July 29, driving oil prices sharply higher and making XLE the top-performing sector in Q3 — providing a natural hedge against a tech-book drawdown.
ideas.bear_case: Iran tensions de-escalate unexpectedly or a US-brokered ceasefire collapses the geopolitical risk premium; FOMC holds with a dovish tone and falling oil prices pressure the entire energy sector lower.
